NKJV.1: Does not wisdom cry out, And understanding lift up her voice?
NKJV.2: She takes her stand on the top of the high hill, Beside the way, where the paths meet.
NKJV.3: She cries out by the gates, at the entry of the city, At the entrance of the doors:
NKJV.4: “To you, O men, I call, And my voice is to the sons of men.
NKJV.5: O you simple ones, understand prudence, And you fools, be of an understanding heart.
NKJV.6: Listen, for I will speak of excellent things, And from the opening of my lips will come right things;
NKJV.7: For my mouth will speak truth; Wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
NKJV.8: All the words of my mouth are with righteousness; Nothing crooked or perverse is in them.
NKJV.9: They are all plain to him who understands, And right to those who find knowledge.
NKJV.10: Receive my instruction, and not silver, And knowledge rather than choice gold;
NKJV.11: For wisdom is better than rubies, And all the things one may desire cannot be compared with her.
NKJV.12: “I, wisdom, dwell with prudence, And find out knowledge and discretion.
NKJV.13: The fear of the LORD is to hate evil; Pride and arrogance and the evil way And the perverse mouth I hate.
NKJV.14: Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom; I am understanding, I have strength.
NKJV.15: By me kings reign, And rulers decree justice.
NKJV.16: By me princes rule, and nobles, All the judges of the earth.
NKJV.17: I love those who love me, And those who seek me diligently will find me.
NKJV.18: Riches and honor are with me, Enduring riches and righteousness.
NKJV.19: My fruit is better than gold, yes, than fine gold, And my revenue than choice silver.
NKJV.20: I traverse the way of righteousness, In the midst of the paths of justice,
NKJV.21: That I may cause those who love me to inherit wealth, That I may fill their treasuries.
NKJV.22: “The  LORD possessed me at the beginning of His way, Before His works of old.
NKJV.23: I have been established from everlasting, From the beginning, before there was ever an earth.
NKJV.24: When there were no depths I was brought forth, When there were no fountains abounding with water.
NKJV.25: Before the mountains were settled, Before the hills, I was brought forth;
NKJV.26: While as yet He had not made the earth or the fields, Or the primal dust of the world.
NKJV.27: When He prepared the heavens, I was there, When He drew a circle on the face of the deep,
NKJV.28: When He established the clouds above, When He strengthened the fountains of the deep,
NKJV.29: When He assigned to the sea its limit, So that the waters would not transgress His command, When He marked out the foundations of the earth,
NKJV.30: Then I was beside Him as a master craftsman; And I was daily His delight, Rejoicing always before Him,
NKJV.31: Rejoicing in His inhabited world, And my delight was with the sons of men.
NKJV.32: “Now therefore, listen to me, my children, For blessed are those who keep my ways.
NKJV.33: Hear instruction and be wise, And do not disdain it.
NKJV.34: Blessed is the man who listens to me, Watching daily at my gates, Waiting at the posts of my doors.
NKJV.35: For whoever finds me finds life, And obtains favor from the LORD;
NKJV.36: But he who sins against me wrongs his own soul; All those who hate me love death.”

ESV.1: Does not wisdom call? Does not understanding raise her voice?
ESV.2: On the heights beside the way, at the crossroads she takes her stand;
ESV.3: beside the gates in front of the town, at the entrance of the portals she cries aloud:
ESV.4: “To you, O men, I call, and my cry is to the children of man.
ESV.5: O simple ones, learn prudence; O fools, learn sense.
ESV.6: Hear, for I will speak noble things, and from my lips will come what is right,
ESV.7: for my mouth will utter truth; wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
ESV.8: All the words of my mouth are righteous; there is nothing twisted or crooked in them.
ESV.9: They are all straight to him who understands, and right to those who find knowledge.
ESV.10: Take my instruction instead of silver, and knowledge rather than choice gold,
ESV.11: for wisdom is better than jewels, and all that you may desire cannot compare with her.
ESV.12: “I, wisdom, dwell with prudence, and I find knowledge and discretion.
ESV.13: The fear of the LORD is hatred of evil. Pride and arrogance and the way of evil and perverted speech I hate.
ESV.14: I have counsel and sound wisdom; I have insight; I have strength.
ESV.15: By me kings reign, and rulers decree what is just;
ESV.16: by me princes rule, and nobles, all who govern justly.
ESV.17: I love those who love me, and those who seek me diligently find me.
ESV.18: Riches and honor are with me, enduring wealth and righteousness.
ESV.19: My fruit is better than gold, even fine gold, and my yield than choice silver.
ESV.20: I walk in the way of righteousness, in the paths of justice,
ESV.21: granting an inheritance to those who love me, and filling their treasuries.
ESV.22: “The LORD possessed  me at the beginning of his work,  the first of his acts of old.
ESV.23: Ages ago I was set up, at the first, before the beginning of the earth.
ESV.24: When there were no depths I was brought forth, when there were no springs abounding with water.
ESV.25: Before the mountains had been shaped, before the hills, I was brought forth,
ESV.26: before he had made the earth with its fields, or the first of the dust of the world.
ESV.27: When he established the heavens, I was there; when he drew a circle on the face of the deep,
ESV.28: when he made firm the skies above, when he established  the fountains of the deep,
ESV.29: when he assigned to the sea its limit, so that the waters might not transgress his command, when he marked out the foundations of the earth,
ESV.30: then I was beside him, like a master workman, and I was daily his  delight, rejoicing before him always,
ESV.31: rejoicing in his inhabited world and delighting in the children of man.
ESV.32: “And now, O sons, listen to me: blessed are those who keep my ways.
ESV.33: Hear instruction and be wise, and do not neglect it.
ESV.34: Blessed is the one who listens to me, watching daily at my gates, waiting beside my doors.
ESV.35: For whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the LORD,
ESV.36: but he who fails to find me injures himself; all who hate me love death.”

NASB.1: Does not wisdom call, And understanding lift up her voice?
NASB.2: On top of the heights beside the way, Where the paths meet, she takes her stand;
NASB.3: Beside the gates, at the opening to the city, At the entrance of the doors, she cries out:
NASB.4: 'To you, O men, I call, And my voice is to the sons of men.
NASB.5: 'O naive ones, understand prudence; And, O fools, understand wisdom.
NASB.6: 'Listen, for I will speak noble things; And the opening of my lips [will reveal] right things.
NASB.7: 'For my mouth will utter truth; And wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
NASB.8: 'All the utterances of my mouth are in righteousness; There is nothing crooked or perverted in them.
NASB.9: 'They are all straightforward to him who understands, And right to those who find knowledge.
NASB.10: 'Take my instruction and not silver, And knowledge rather than choicest gold.
NASB.11: 'For wisdom is better than jewels; And all desirable things cannot compare with her.
NASB.12: 'I, wisdom, dwell with prudence, And I find knowledge [and] discretion.
NASB.13: 'The fear of the LORD is to hate evil; Pride and arrogance and the evil way And the perverted mouth, I hate.
NASB.14: 'Counsel is mine and sound wisdom; I am understanding, power is mine.
NASB.15: 'By me kings reign, And rulers decree justice.
NASB.16: 'By me princes rule, and nobles, All who judge rightly.
NASB.17: 'I love those who love me; And those who diligently seek me will find me.
NASB.18: 'Riches and honor are with me, Enduring wealth and righteousness.
NASB.19: 'My fruit is better than gold, even pure gold, And my yield [better] than choicest silver.
NASB.20: 'I walk in the way of righteousness, In the midst of the paths of justice,
NASB.21: To endow those who love me with wealth, That I may fill their treasuries.
NASB.22: 'The LORD possessed me at the beginning of His way, Before His works of old.
NASB.23: 'From everlasting I was established, From the beginning, from the earliest times of the earth.
NASB.24: 'When there were no depths I was brought forth, When there were no springs abounding with water.
NASB.25: 'Before the mountains were settled, Before the hills I was brought forth;
NASB.26: While He had not yet made the earth and the fields, Nor the first dust of the world.
NASB.27: 'When He established the heavens, I was there, When He inscribed a circle on the face of the deep,
NASB.28: When He made firm the skies above, When the springs of the deep became fixed,
NASB.29: When He set for the sea its boundary So that the water would not transgress His command, When He marked out the foundations of the earth;
NASB.30: Then I was beside Him, [as] a master workman; And I was daily [His] delight, Rejoicing always before Him,
NASB.31: Rejoicing in the world, His earth, And [having] my delight in the sons of men.
NASB.32: 'Now therefore, [O] sons, listen to me, For blessed are they who keep my ways.
NASB.33: 'Heed instruction and be wise, And do not neglect [it].
NASB.34: 'Blessed is the man who listens to me, Watching daily at my gates, Waiting at my doorposts.
NASB.35: 'For he who finds me finds life And obtains favor from the LORD.
NASB.36: 'But he who sins against me injures himself; All those who hate me love death.'

NLT.1:
Listen as Wisdom calls out!
Hear as understanding raises her voice!
NLT.2:
On the hilltop along the road,
she takes her stand at the crossroads.
NLT.3:
By the gates at the entrance to the town,
on the road leading in, she cries aloud,
NLT.4:
“I call to you, to all of you!
I raise my voice to all people.
NLT.5:
You simple people, use good judgment.
You foolish people, show some understanding.
NLT.6:
Listen to me! For I have important things to tell you.
Everything I say is right,
NLT.7:
for I speak the truth
and detest every kind of deception.
NLT.8:
My advice is wholesome.
There is nothing devious or crooked in it.
NLT.9:
My words are plain to anyone with understanding,
clear to those with knowledge.
NLT.10:
Choose my instruction rather than silver,
and knowledge rather than pure gold.
NLT.11:
For wisdom is far more valuable than rubies.
Nothing you desire can compare with it.
NLT.12:
“I, Wisdom, live together with good judgment.
I know where to discover knowledge and discernment.
NLT.13:
All who fear the LORD will hate evil.
Therefore, I hate pride and arrogance,
corruption and perverse speech.
NLT.14:
Common sense and success belong to me.
Insight and strength are mine.
NLT.15:
Because of me, kings reign,
and rulers make just decrees.
NLT.16:
Rulers lead with my help,
and nobles make righteous judgments.
NLT.17:
“I love all who love me.
Those who search will surely find me.
NLT.18:
I have riches and honor,
as well as enduring wealth and justice.
NLT.19:
My gifts are better than gold, even the purest gold,
my wages better than sterling silver!
NLT.20:
I walk in righteousness,
in paths of justice.
NLT.21:
Those who love me inherit wealth.
I will fill their treasuries.
NLT.22:
“The LORD formed me from the beginning,
before he created anything else.
NLT.23:
I was appointed in ages past,
at the very first, before the earth began.
NLT.24:
I was born before the oceans were created,
before the springs bubbled forth their waters.
NLT.25:
Before the mountains were formed,
before the hills, I was born —
NLT.26:
before he had made the earth and fields
and the first handfuls of soil.
NLT.27:
I was there when he established the heavens,
when he drew the horizon on the oceans.
NLT.28:
I was there when he set the clouds above,
when he established springs deep in the earth.
NLT.29:
I was there when he set the limits of the seas,
so they would not spread beyond their boundaries.
And when he marked off the earth’s foundations,
NLT.30:
I was the architect at his side.
I was his constant delight,
rejoicing always in his presence.
NLT.31:
And how happy I was with the world he created;
how I rejoiced with the human family!
NLT.32:
“And so, my children, listen to me,
for all who follow my ways are joyful.
NLT.33:
Listen to my instruction and be wise.
Don’t ignore it.
NLT.34:
Joyful are those who listen to me,
watching for me daily at my gates,
waiting for me outside my home!
NLT.35:
For whoever finds me finds life
and receives favor from the LORD.
NLT.36:
But those who miss me injure themselves.
All who hate me love death.”

WEB.1: Doesn’t wisdom cry out? Doesn’t understanding raise her voice?
WEB.2: On the top of high places by the way, where the paths meet, she stands.
WEB.3: Beside the gates, at the entry of the city, at the entry doors, she cries aloud:
WEB.4: “To you men, I call! I send my voice to the sons of mankind.
WEB.5: You simple, understand prudence. You fools, be of an understanding heart.
WEB.6: Hear, for I will speak excellent things. The opening of my lips is for right things.
WEB.7: For my mouth speaks truth. Wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
WEB.8: All the words of my mouth are in righteousness. There is nothing crooked or perverse in them.
WEB.9: They are all plain to him who understands, right to those who find knowledge.
WEB.10: Receive my instruction rather than silver; knowledge rather than choice gold.
WEB.11: For wisdom is better than rubies. All the things that may be desired can’t be compared to it.
WEB.12: “I, wisdom, have made prudence my dwelling. Find out knowledge and discretion.
WEB.13: The fear of Yahweh is to hate evil. I hate pride, arrogance, the evil way, and the perverse mouth.
WEB.14: Counsel and sound knowledge are mine. I have understanding and power.
WEB.15: By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.
WEB.16: By me princes rule; nobles, and all the righteous rulers of the earth.
WEB.17: I love those who love me. Those who seek me diligently will find me.
WEB.18: With me are riches, honor, enduring wealth, and prosperity.
WEB.19: My fruit is better than gold, yes, than fine gold; my yield than choice silver.
WEB.20: I walk in the way of righteousness, in the middle of the paths of justice;
WEB.21: That I may give wealth to those who love me. I fill their treasuries.
WEB.22: “Yahweh possessed me in the beginning of his work, before his deeds of old.
WEB.23: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, before the earth existed.
WEB.24: When there were no depths, I was born, when there were no springs abounding with water.
WEB.25: Before the mountains were settled in place, before the hills, I was born;
WEB.26: while as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the beginning of the dust of the world.
WEB.27: When he established the heavens, I was there; when he set a circle on the surface of the deep,
WEB.28: when he established the clouds above, when the springs of the deep became strong,
WEB.29: when he gave to the sea its boundary, that the waters should not violate his commandment, when he marked out the foundations of the earth;
WEB.30: then I was the craftsman by his side. I was a delight day by day, always rejoicing before him,
WEB.31: Rejoicing in his whole world. My delight was with the sons of men.
WEB.32: “Now therefore, my sons, listen to me, for blessed are those who keep my ways.
WEB.33: Hear instruction, and be wise. Don’t refuse it.
WEB.34: Blessed is the man who hears me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at my door posts.
WEB.35: For whoever finds me, finds life, and will obtain favor from Yahweh.
WEB.36: But he who sins against me wrongs his own soul. All those who hate me love death.”

at the crossroads, she takes her stand.

“People, I call out to you;
my cry is to the children of Adam.

Learn to be shrewd, you who are inexperienced;
develop common sense, you who are foolish.

lasting wealth and righteousness. ⓜ

I walk in the ways of righteousness,
along the paths of justice,
CSB17.21:
giving wealth as an inheritance to those who love me,
and filling their treasuries.
CSB17.22:
“The LORD acquired [3] me
at the beginning of his creation, [4]
before his works of long ago. ⓐ
CSB17.23:
I was formed [5] before ancient times,
from the beginning, before the earth began. ⓑ
CSB17.24:
I was born
when there were no watery depths ⓒ
and no springs filled with water.
CSB17.25:
Before the mountains were established,
prior to the hills, ⓓ I was given birth —
CSB17.26:
before he made the land, the fields,
or the first soil on earth.
CSB17.27:
I was there when he established the heavens, ⓔ
when he laid out the horizon on the surface of the ocean, ⓕ
CSB17.28:
when he placed the skies above,
when the fountains of the ocean gushed out,
CSB17.29:
when he set a limit for the sea
so that the waters would not violate his command, ⓖ
when he laid out the foundations of the earth. ⓗ
CSB17.30:
I was a skilled craftsman [6] beside him. ⓘ
I was his [7] delight every day,
always rejoicing before him.
CSB17.31:
I was rejoicing in his inhabited world, ⓙ
delighting in the children of Adam. ⓚ
CSB17.32:
“And now, sons, listen to me;
those who keep my ways are happy. ⓛ
CSB17.33:
Listen to instruction and be wise; ⓜ
don’t ignore it.
CSB17.34:
Anyone who listens to me is happy, ⓝ
watching at my doors every day,
waiting by the posts of my doorway.
CSB17.35:
For the one who finds me finds life ⓐ
and obtains favor from the LORD, ⓑ
CSB17.36:
but the one who misses me [8] harms himself; ⓒ
all who hate me love death.” ⓓ

AMP.1:
Does not wisdom call,
And understanding lift up her voice?
AMP.2:
On the top of the heights beside the way,
Where the paths meet, wisdom takes her stand;
AMP.3:
Beside the gates, at the entrance to the city,
At the entrance of the doors, she cries out:
AMP.4:
"To you, O men, I call,
And my voice is directed to the sons of men.
AMP.5:
"O you naive or inexperienced [who are easily misled], understand prudence and seek astute common sense;
And, O you [closed-minded, self-confident] fools, understand wisdom [seek the insight and self-discipline that leads to godly living].
AMP.6:
"Listen, for I will speak excellent and noble things;
And the opening of my lips will reveal right things.
AMP.7:
"For my mouth will utter truth,
And wickedness is repulsive and loathsome to my lips.
AMP.8:
"All the words of my mouth are in righteousness (upright, in right standing with God);
There is nothing contrary to truth or perverted (crooked) in them.
AMP.9:
"They are all straightforward to him who understands [with an open and willing mind],
And right to those who find knowledge and live by it.
AMP.10:
"Take my instruction rather than [seeking] silver,
And take knowledge rather than choicest gold,
AMP.11:
"For wisdom is better than rubies;
And all desirable things cannot compare with her.
AMP.12:
"I, [godly] wisdom, reside with prudence [good judgment, moral courage and astute common sense],
And I find knowledge and discretion.
AMP.13:
"The [reverent] fear and worshipful awe of the LORD includes the hatred of evil;
Pride and arrogance and the evil way,
And the perverted mouth, I hate.
AMP.14:
"Counsel is mine and sound wisdom;
I am understanding, power and strength are mine.
AMP.15:
"By me kings reign
And rulers decide and decree justice.
AMP.16:
"By me princes rule, and nobles,
All who judge and govern rightly.
AMP.17:
"I love those who love me;
And those who seek me early and diligently will find me.
AMP.18:
"Riches and honor are with me,
Enduring wealth and righteousness (right standing with God).
AMP.19:
"My fruit is better than gold, even pure gold,
And my yield is better than choicest silver.
AMP.20:
"I, [Wisdom, continuously] walk in the way of righteousness,
In the midst of the paths of justice,
AMP.21:
That I may cause those who love me to inherit wealth and true riches,
And that I may fill their treasuries.
AMP.22:
"The LORD created and possessed me at the beginning of His way,
Before His works of old [were accomplished].
AMP.23:
"From everlasting I was established and ordained,
From the beginning, before the earth existed, [I, godly wisdom, existed].
AMP.24:
"When there were no ocean depths I was born,
When there were no fountains and springs overflowing with water.
AMP.25:
"Before the mountains were settled,
Before the hills, I was born;
AMP.26:
While He had not yet made the earth and the fields,
Or the first of the dust of the earth.
AMP.27:
"When He established the heavens, I [Wisdom] was there;
When He drew a circle upon the face of the deep,
AMP.28:
When He made firm the skies above,
When the fountains and springs of the deep became fixed and strong,
AMP.29:
When He set for the sea its boundary
So that the waters would not transgress [the boundaries set by] His command,
When He marked out the foundations of the earth—
AMP.30:
Then I was beside Him, as a master craftsman;
And I was daily His delight;
Rejoicing before Him always,
AMP.31:
Rejoicing in the world, His inhabited earth,
And having my delight in the sons of men.
AMP.32:
"Now therefore, O sons, listen to me,
For blessed [happy, prosperous, to be admired] are they who keep my ways.
AMP.33:
"Heed (pay attention to) instruction and be wise,
And do not ignore or neglect it.
AMP.34:
"Blessed [happy, prosperous, to be admired] is the man who listens to me,
Watching daily at my gates,
Waiting at my doorposts.
AMP.35:
"For whoever finds me (Wisdom) finds life
And obtains favor and grace from the LORD.
AMP.36:
"But he who fails to find me or sins against me injures himself;
All those who hate me love and court death."

MSG.1:
Do you hear Lady Wisdom calling?
Can you hear Madame Insight raising her voice?
MSG.2:
She's taken her stand at First and Main,
at the busiest intersection.
MSG.3:
Right in the city square
where the traffic is thickest, she shouts,
MSG.4:
"You—I'm talking to all of you,
everyone out here on the streets!
MSG.5:
Listen, you idiots—learn good sense!
You blockheads—shape up!
MSG.6:
Don't miss a word of this—I'm telling you how to live well,
I'm telling you how to live at your best.
MSG.7:
My mouth chews and savors and relishes truth—
I can't stand the taste of evil!
MSG.8:
You'll only hear true and right words from my mouth;
not one syllable will be twisted or skewed.
MSG.9:
You'll recognize this as true—you with open minds;
truth-ready minds will see it at once.
MSG.10:
Prefer my life-disciplines over chasing after money,
and God-knowledge over a lucrative career.
MSG.11:
For Wisdom is better than all the trappings of wealth;
nothing you could wish for holds a candle to her.
MSG.12:
"I am Lady Wisdom, and I live next to Sanity;
Knowledge and Discretion live just down the street.
MSG.13:
The Fear-of-God means hating Evil,
whose ways I hate with a passion—
pride and arrogance and crooked talk.
MSG.14:
Good counsel and common sense are my characteristics;
I am both Insight and the Virtue to live it out.
MSG.15:
With my help, leaders rule,
and lawmakers legislate fairly;
MSG.16:
With my help, governors govern,
along with all in legitimate authority.
MSG.17:
I love those who love me;
those who look for me find me.
MSG.18:
Wealth and Glory accompany me—
also substantial Honor and a Good Name.
MSG.19:
My benefits are worth more than a big salary, even a very big salary;
the returns on me exceed any imaginable bonus.
MSG.20:
You can find me on Righteous Road—that's where I walk—
at the intersection of Justice Avenue,
MSG.21:
Handing out life to those who love me,
filling their arms with life—armloads of life!
MSG.22:
"God sovereignly made me—the first, the basic—
before he did anything else.
MSG.23:
I was brought into being a long time ago,
well before Earth got its start.
MSG.24:
I arrived on the scene before Ocean,
yes, even before Springs and Rivers and Lakes.
MSG.25:
Before Mountains were sculpted and Hills took shape,
I was already there, newborn;
MSG.26:
Long before God stretched out Earth's Horizons,
and tended to the minute details of Soil and Weather,
MSG.27:
And set Sky firmly in place,
I was there.
When he mapped and gave borders to wild Ocean,
MSG.28:
built the vast vault of Heaven,
and installed the fountains that fed Ocean,
MSG.29:
When he drew a boundary for Sea,
posted a sign that said, no trespassing,
And then staked out Earth's foundations,
MSG.30:
I was right there with him, making sure everything fit.
Day after day I was there, with my joyful applause,
always enjoying his company,
MSG.31:
Delighted with the world of things and creatures,
happily celebrating the human family.
MSG.32:
"So, my dear friends, listen carefully;
those who embrace these my ways are most blessed.
MSG.33:
Mark a life of discipline and live wisely;
don't squander your precious life.
MSG.34:
Blessed the man, blessed the woman, who listens to me,
awake and ready for me each morning,
alert and responsive as I start my day's work.
MSG.35:
When you find me, you find life, real life,
to say nothing of God's good pleasure.
MSG.36:
But if you wrong me, you damage your very soul;
when you reject me, you're flirting with death."

NIV.1:
Wisdom’s Call
Does not wisdom call out?
Does not understanding raise her voice?
NIV.2:
On the heights along the way,
where the paths meet, she takes her stand;
NIV.3:
beside the gates leading into the city,
at the entrances, she cries aloud:
NIV.4:
“To you, O men, I call out;
I raise my voice to all mankind.
NIV.5:
You who are simple, gain prudence;
you who are foolish, gain understanding.
NIV.6:
Listen, for I have worthy things to say;
I open my lips to speak what is right.
NIV.7:
My mouth speaks what is true,
for my lips detest wickedness.
NIV.8:
All the words of my mouth are just;
none of them is crooked or perverse.
NIV.9:
To the discerning all of them are right;
they are faultless to those who have knowledge.
NIV.10:
Choose my instruction instead of silver,
knowledge rather than choice gold,
NIV.11:
for wisdom is more precious than rubies,
and nothing you desire can compare with her.
NIV.12:
“I, wisdom, dwell together with prudence;
I possess knowledge and discretion.
NIV.13:
To fear the Lord is to hate evil;
I hate pride and arrogance,
evil behavior and perverse speech.
NIV.14:
Counsel and sound judgment are mine;
I have understanding and power.
NIV.15:
By me kings reign
and rulers make laws that are just;
NIV.16:
by me princes govern,
and all nobles who rule on earth.
NIV.17:
I love those who love me,
and those who seek me find me.
NIV.18:
With me are riches and honor,
enduring wealth and prosperity.
NIV.19:
My fruit is better than fine gold;
what I yield surpasses choice silver.
NIV.20:
I walk in the way of righteousness,
along the paths of justice,
NIV.21:
bestowing wealth on those who love me
and making their treasuries full.
NIV.22:
“The Lord brought me forth as the first of his works,
before his deeds of old;
NIV.23:
I was appointed from eternity,
from the beginning, before the world began.
NIV.24:
When there were no oceans, I was given birth,
when there were no springs abounding with water;
NIV.25:
before the mountains were settled in place,
before the hills, I was given birth,
NIV.26:
before he made the earth or its fields
or any of the dust of the world.
NIV.27:
I was there when he set the heavens in place,
when he marked out the horizon on the face of the deep,
NIV.28:
when he established the clouds above
and fixed securely the fountains of the deep,
NIV.29:
when he gave the sea its boundary
so the waters would not overstep his command,
and when he marked out the foundations of the earth.
NIV.30:
Then I was the craftsman at his side.
I was filled with delight day after day,
rejoicing always in his presence,
NIV.31:
rejoicing in his whole world
and delighting in mankind.
NIV.32:
“Now then, my sons, listen to me;
blessed are those who keep my ways.
NIV.33:
Listen to my instruction and be wise;
do not ignore it.
NIV.34:
Blessed is the man who listens to me,
watching daily at my doors,
waiting at my doorway.
NIV.35:
For whoever finds me finds life
and receives favor from the Lord.
NIV.36:
But whoever fails to find me harms himself;
all who hate me love death.”

NRSVCE.1:
Does not wisdom call,
and does not understanding raise her voice?
NRSVCE.2:
On the heights, beside the way,
at the crossroads she takes her stand;
NRSVCE.3:
beside the gates in front of the town,
at the entrance of the portals she cries out:
NRSVCE.4:
“To you, O people, I call,
and my cry is to all that live.
NRSVCE.5:
O simple ones, learn prudence;
acquire intelligence, you who lack it.
NRSVCE.6:
Hear, for I will speak noble things,
and from my lips will come what is right;
NRSVCE.7:
for my mouth will utter truth;
wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
NRSVCE.8:
All the words of my mouth are righteous;
there is nothing twisted or crooked in them.
NRSVCE.9:
They are all straight to one who understands
and right to those who find knowledge.
NRSVCE.10:
Take my instruction instead of silver,
and knowledge rather than choice gold;
NRSVCE.11:
for wisdom is better than jewels,
and all that you may desire cannot compare with her.
NRSVCE.12:
I, wisdom, live with prudence,
and I attain knowledge and discretion.
NRSVCE.13:
The fear of the LORD is hatred of evil.
Pride and arrogance and the way of evil
and perverted speech I hate.
NRSVCE.14:
I have good advice and sound wisdom;
I have insight, I have strength.
NRSVCE.15:
By me kings reign,
and rulers decree what is just;
NRSVCE.16:
by me rulers rule,
and nobles, all who govern rightly.
NRSVCE.17:
I love those who love me,
and those who seek me diligently find me.
NRSVCE.18:
Riches and honor are with me,
enduring wealth and prosperity.
NRSVCE.19:
My fruit is better than gold, even fine gold,
and my yield than choice silver.
NRSVCE.20:
I walk in the way of righteousness,
along the paths of justice,
NRSVCE.21:
endowing with wealth those who love me,
and filling their treasuries.
NRSVCE.22:
The LORD created me at the beginning of his work,
the first of his acts of long ago.
NRSVCE.23:
Ages ago I was set up,
at the first, before the beginning of the earth.
NRSVCE.24:
When there were no depths I was brought forth,
when there were no springs abounding with water.
NRSVCE.25:
Before the mountains had been shaped,
before the hills, I was brought forth —
NRSVCE.26:
when he had not yet made earth and fields,
or the world’s first bits of soil.
NRSVCE.27:
When he established the heavens, I was there,
when he drew a circle on the face of the deep,
NRSVCE.28:
when he made firm the skies above,
when he established the fountains of the deep,
NRSVCE.29:
when he assigned to the sea its limit,
so that the waters might not transgress his command,
when he marked out the foundations of the earth,
NRSVCE.30:
then I was beside him, like a master worker;
and I was daily his delight,
rejoicing before him always,
NRSVCE.31:
rejoicing in his inhabited world
and delighting in the human race.
NRSVCE.32:
“And now, my children, listen to me:
happy are those who keep my ways.
NRSVCE.33:
Hear instruction and be wise,
and do not neglect it.
NRSVCE.34:
Happy is the one who listens to me,
watching daily at my gates,
waiting beside my doors.
NRSVCE.35:
For whoever finds me finds life
and obtains favor from the LORD;
NRSVCE.36:
but those who miss me injure themselves;
all who hate me love death.”

NET.1: Does not wisdom call out? Does not understanding raise her voice?
NET.2: At the top of the elevated places along the way, at the intersection of the paths she takes her stand;
NET.3: beside the gates opening into the city, at the entrance of the doorways she cries out:
NET.4: “To you, O people, I call out, and my voice calls to all mankind.
NET.5: You who are naive, discern wisdom! And you fools, understand discernment!
NET.6: Listen, for I will speak excellent things, and my lips will utter what is right.
NET.7: For my mouth speaks truth, and my lips hate wickedness.
NET.8: All the words of my mouth are righteous; there is nothing in them twisted or crooked.
NET.9: All of them are clear to the discerning and upright to those who find knowledge.
NET.10: Receive my instruction rather than silver, and knowledge rather than choice gold.
NET.11: For wisdom is better than rubies, and desirable things cannot be compared to her.
NET.12: “I, wisdom, live with prudence, and I find knowledge and discretion.
NET.13: The fear of the LORD is to hate evil; I hate arrogant pride and the evil way and perverse utterances.
NET.14: Counsel and sound wisdom belong to me; I possess understanding and might.
NET.15: Kings reign by means of me, and potentates decree righteousness;
NET.16: by me princes rule, as well as nobles and all righteous judges.
NET.17: I love those who love me, and those who seek me find me.
NET.18: Riches and honor are with me, long-lasting wealth and righteousness.
NET.19: My fruit is better than the purest gold, and what I produce is better than choice silver.
NET.20: I walk in the path of righteousness, in the pathway of justice,
NET.21: that I may cause those who love me to inherit wealth, and that I may fill their treasuries.
NET.22: The LORD created me as the beginning of his works, before his deeds of long ago.
NET.23: From eternity I was appointed, from the beginning, from before the world existed.
NET.24: When there were no deep oceans I was born, when there were no springs overflowing with water;
NET.25: before the mountains were set in place — before the hills — I was born,
NET.26: before he made the earth and its fields, or the beginning of the dust of the world.
NET.27: When he established the heavens, I was there; when he marked out the horizon over the face of the deep,
NET.28: when he established the clouds above, when the fountains of the deep grew strong,
NET.29: when he gave the sea his decree that the waters should not pass over his command, when he marked out the foundations of the earth,
NET.30: then I was beside him as a master craftsman, and I was his delight day by day, rejoicing before him at all times,
NET.31: rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth, and delighting in its people.
NET.32: “So now, children, listen to me; blessed are those who keep my ways.
NET.33: Listen to my instruction so that you may be wise, and do not neglect it.
NET.34: Blessed is the one who listens to me, watching at my doors day by day, waiting beside my doorway.
NET.35: For the one who finds me finds life and receives favor from the LORD.
NET.36: But the one who does not find me brings harm to himself; all who hate me love death.”
